mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth reduces equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between GDPC1 quarterly growth and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate for future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive or neutral in >50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises >0.25pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in >40% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 900 thousand units signals weakening aggregate demand and construction activity, which drags down industrial and consumer sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 900 in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing and mining output, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL accelerates by >0.5pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving consumer credit combined with stable or rising housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and spending capacity, fueling corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising RSAFS and non-declining HOUST fails to yield positive 5-day SPX returns in >50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate alongside slowing payroll growth signals an impending economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive when UNRATE rises >0.4pp in two months despite PAYEMS deceleration.
